learning_outcomes.description: Safely and competently restrain and examine healthy, injured and diseased animals, including domestic and exotic species, and fauna.

Demonstrate appropriate knowledge and identify skills applicable to veterinary technology and nursing practice in the context of well, injured, and diseased animals across the fields of anaesthesia, surgery, radiography, dentistry, general medicine, physiotherapy, clinical nutrition, oncology and avian/exotics care.
Demonstrate an appropriate level of competency in all aspects of surgical nursing, including asepsis, patient preparation, personnel preparation/management, and instrumentation.
Work with colleagues as a team to communicate concepts and accomplish shared goals and reflect on the performance of team members.

assessments.task_description:
The Veterinary Terminology Workbook introduces students to a range of terms relevant to veterinary technology and nursing in a contextualised format. 

Objectives:

By the end of this task, students should be able to:

  • Identify prefixes, suffixes and roots that make up common veterinary medical terms
  • Demonstrate an understanding of a variety of basic veterinary medical terms
  • Appreciate the importance of learning medical terminology for employment within the veterinary and allied animal health industries


Instructions:

Students will be given an online Terminology Workbook at the start of the semester and are required to work through each of the TEN (10) Sections, completing TWO (2) sections each week (or by Week 8 at the latest). To complete this task, students will need to refer to the on-line notes provided on Blackboard. An in-person, online, In-Semester Exam will be scheduled for Week 10 - further details of this exam will be made available on Bb and discussed during Week 1.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessments.task_description:
Students are required to attend and engage in 3 TBL Assessments.

TBL 1: Anaesthesia (5%) - In-class assessment of engagement and completion of individual and group tasks.

Students will complete an individual quiz based on course content. Students will then work in groups to undertake a second quiz. Marks will be an average of the individual and group quiz results.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person.


TBL 2: Radiography (5%) - In-class assessment of engagement and completion of individual and group tasks.

Students will complete an individual quiz based on course content. Students will then work in groups to undertake a series of patient positioning techniques that allow for radiographs to be taken effectively, this will culminate in a group assessment of ONE (1) position, chosen at random for each group. Marks will be an average of the individual quiz result and group activity results.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person.


TBL 3: Avian & Exotic Patient Care (5%) - In-class assessment of engagement and completion of group task.

Students will work in groups to create an information sheet that can be provided to hypothetical pet owners covering an assigned disease, its impact on husbandry, behaviour, nutrition and ways to improve their avian or exotic pets' health. Groups will be allocated randomly on the day of the Workshop. Assessment of this task will include peer evaluation of in-group engagement.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person.

assessments.task_description:
This is an Objective Structured Clinical Examination based on course content designed to assess students' ability to perform various clinical and technical tasks. This will be held in the Centrally Scheduled Exam Pariod.

Students will progress through a number of stations where they will be required to perform tasks and demonstrate skills taught/acquired in their practical classes. Students may also be expected to answer questions related to the task being examined in written or verbal form. All students will be examined on the same day.

Assessed tasks may include:

  • Behaviour, handling and restraint
  • Clinical exam
  • Catheter placement (model)
  • Blood Draw (model)
  • Preparing patients for surgery 
  • Dentistry (model)
  • Instruments (dentistry & surgery)
  • PALACE - R (radiography)

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will undertake a written examination comprised of multiple choice questions, short answer and problem-solving questions to demonstrate an understanding of VETS2043 content. Examinable materials will be from any lectures, practical classes and other content supplied.

Inspera Assessment may be used in VETS2043; this is an invigilated on-campus digital exam and students in this course will complete using their own device. If own device is not available, student services is available to help organise alternative devices for use. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided in the Blackboard site for this course during the semester.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 9(27 Jul - 27 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Lecture Series

A series of lectures covering foundations of nursing methods, anaesthesia, radiology, care of the surgical patient (including detailed explanations of various surgical procedures), wound management and care.
learning_activities.topic_title: Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Lecture Series
learning_activities.topic_description: Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Lecture Series A series of lectures covering foundations of nursing methods, anaesthesia, radiology, care of the surgical patient (including detailed explanations of various surgical procedures), wound management and care.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 9(03 Aug - 27 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Practical Series

Wound Management and Care Practical: Includes practical sessions on surgical preparation of wounds and bandaging of limbs in dogs/cats/horses/livestock.

IV Catheter Placement and Blood Draw Practical: Using models students will increase skills using muscle memory to place catheters and complete blood draws.

Escape Rooms: An interactive Team-Based learning experience that draws on knowledge and skills from the lecture series.
learning_activities.topic_title: Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Practical Series
learning_activities.topic_description: Methods of Nursing Practice for the Veterinary Technologist - Practical Series Wound Management and Care Practical: Includes practical sessions on surgical preparation of wounds and bandaging of limbs in dogs/cats/horses/livestock. Escape Rooms: An interactive Team-Based learning experience that draws on knowledge and skills from the lecture series.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 13(10 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Para-Veterinary Disciplines - Practical Series

Physiotherapy Practical: students will learn to apply the theory of physiotherapy modalities to companion animals.

Dentistry Practical: using models, students will increase their skills in charting, scaling and cleaning in companion animal patients.

Nutrition Applied Case Practical: students will be emersed in case-based companion animal nutrition scenarios.

learning_activities.topic:
Para-Veterinary Disciplines - Lecture Series

Para-Veterinary Lecture Series: builds knowledge and skills in the para-veterinary disciplines of fluid therapy, dentistry, oncology, clinical nutrition and physiotherapy.

learning_activities.topic:
Avian and Exotics - Lecture Series

Avian and Exotics Lecture Series: builds knowledge and skills in reptile, avian, mammal and native fauna nursing.
